context: Project has a legacy .claude/bruhs.json with a valid state JSON, but CLAUDE.md does not contain a bruhs:state marker block (the migration hasn't been run). AGENTS.md does not exist.
expected_behavior: Tries CLAUDE.md first — finds no bruhs:state block
Tries AGENTS.md second — file doesn't exist, skip silently
Falls back to .claude/bruhs.json — reads the file and prints its content verbatim to stdout
Prints a stderr warning that mentions the legacy fallback and suggests running /bruhs:claim to migrate — non-fatal, exit code is still 0
Output on stdout is parseable JSON — callers can pipe through `jq` or JSON.parse without preprocessing
failure_modes: Returns exit code 1 / 'block not found' despite the legacy file being present
Prints the warning to stdout, breaking JSON parseability downstream
Hard-fails when AGENTS.md doesn't exist (it should silently skip and try the next)
Reads CLAUDE.md but is fooled into returning the WRONG block kind (e.g. matches a bruhs:rules block when --kind state was requested)
Reads the legacy file even when a valid bruhs:state block IS present in CLAUDE.md (the marker block must take priority)
